deep_dive.title: MARD: Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation for Mechanism-Level Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
deep_dive.abstract: Mechanism-level drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction requires identifying which enzyme or pharmacodynamic axis is implicated, in which direction, and with which evidence -- not merely whether two drugs interact. We introduce a reproducible mechanism-level DDI labelling and evaluation protocol with a structured 7-family/147-subtype taxonomy, leakage-safe cold-split protocols, and auditable reasoning metrics for evaluating pharmacological prediction beyond flat interaction classification. We propose a pipeline that produces a 7B reasoning MARD (Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation), combining three training innovations: a single-token KL divergence on direction tag that ties the model's prediction, per-loss PRM-weighted DPO with programmatic hard negatives, and a leakage-safe mechanism-aware retrieval channel. Process-reward step labels are automatically verifiable against DrugBank-structured fields, requiring no human or LLM judges. On the April-2026 DrugBank release, our MARD-7B is the only system in a 32-system comparison whose accuracy survives drug-pair novelty, beating the best baseline by +13.9 pp and GPT-4o by +6.7 pp at ~1% of frontier API cost. Further analysis reveals an anti-memorisation signature where accuracy improves on rarely seen drugs, suggesting that gain comes from structured pharmacological reasoning rather than drug-frequency memorisation. We release corpus, DDI-PRM, retrieval index, and training code.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: cold-start DDI prediction with LLM reasoning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大型语言模型推理进行冷启动药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用大型语言模型推理进行冷启动药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用大型语言模型推理进行冷启动药物相互作用预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: MARD introduces a mechanism-level DDI prediction pipeline with cold-split evaluation and a 7B reasoning LLM, achieving auditable predictions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对药物相互作用预测中仅分类而缺乏机制解释的问题，提出MARD框架，包含7家族147亚型分类体系、冷隔离划分协议和可审计推理指标。通过镜像增强推理蒸馏训练7B推理模型，结合方向标签的KL散度与PRM加权蒸馏，实现机制级DDI预测。实验表明该方法在冷启动设置下显著优于基线，并提供可解释的推理路径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对药物相互作用预测中仅分类而缺乏机制解释的问题，提出MARD框架，包含7家族147亚型分类体系、冷隔离划分协议和可审计推理指标。通过镜像增强推理蒸馏训练7B推理模型，结合方向标签的KL散度与PRM加权蒸馏，实现机制级DDI预测。实验表明该方法在冷启动设置下显著优于基线，并提供可解释的推理路径。

quick_skim.title: GraspLLM: Towards Zero-Shot Generalization on Text-Attributed Graphs with LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Research on Text-Attributed Graphs (TAGs) has gained significant attention recently due to its broad applications across various real-world data scenarios, such as citation networks, e-commerce platforms, social media, and web pages. Inspired by the remarkable semantic understanding ability of Large Language Models (LLMs), there have been numerous attempts to integrate LLMs into TAGs. However, existing methods still struggle to generalize across diverse graphs and tasks, and their ability to capture transferable graph structural patterns remains limited. To address this, we introduce the GraspLLM, a framework that combines Graph structural comprehension with semantic understanding prowess of LLMs to enhance the cross-dataset and cross-task generalizability. Specifically, we represent node texts from different graphs in a unified semantic space with a frozen general embedding model, on top of which we perform motif-aware contrastive learning across multiple motif-induced adjacency matrices to extract dataset-agnostic structural information. Then, with our proposed optimal contextual subgraph, we extract the most contextually relevant subgraph for each target node and align these subgraphs to the token space of LLM via an alignment projector. Extensive experiments on TAG benchmark datasets spanning diverse domains reveal that GraspLLM consistently outperforms previous LLM-based methods for TAGs, especially in zero-shot scenarios, highlighting its strong generalizability across different datasets and tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/Heinz217/GraspLLM.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: combines graph structural comprehension with LLMs for zero-shot generalization on text-attributed graphs applicable to DDI
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结合图结构理解与大语言模型，实现文本属性图零样本泛化，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结合图结构理解与大语言模型，实现文本属性图零样本泛化，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 结合图结构理解与大语言模型，实现文本属性图零样本泛化，可迁移至药物相互作用预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GraspLLM combines graph structural comprehension with LLM semantic understanding to achieve zero-shot generalization across text-attributed graphs, providing a transferable method for cold-start DDI prediction.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法在文本属性图（如药物节点带有描述）上跨数据集和任务泛化能力不足。本文提出GraspLLM框架，结合图结构理解与大语言模型语义理解，增强零样本泛化能力。实验表明该方法在多个图数据集上实现了优异的跨任务迁移性能。该框架可直接应用于药物-药物相互作用预测中的冷启动问题，具有广泛适用性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有方法在文本属性图（如药物节点带有描述）上跨数据集和任务泛化能力不足。本文提出GraspLLM框架，结合图结构理解与大语言模型语义理解，增强零样本泛化能力。实验表明该方法在多个图数据集上实现了优异的跨任务迁移性能。该框架可直接应用于药物-药物相互作用预测中的冷启动问题，具有广泛适用性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找ColdDDI：利用大型语言模型和生物医学知识图谱进行冷启动药物相互作用预测该方向有关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: Large Language Model + Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction; cold-start drug-drug interaction prediction; large language model for drug interaction; drug interaction prediction with knowledge graph; knowledge graph completion for drug discovery; cold start prediction in drug interaction; Cold start drug drug interaction prediction using large language models and biomedical knowledge graphs; Leveraging knowledge graphs for cold start drug interaction with large language models; Biomedical knowledge graph enhanced drug interaction prediction for new drugs.
